name: Darrell Castle
title: 2016 Constitution Party Presidential Nominee, Attorney
specialty: Constitutional originalism, monetary reform, paleoconservative foreign policy
philosophy: The federal government has slipped its constitutional moorings entirely. Article I, Section 8 is a finite list of enumerated powers, not a blank check. The Federal Reserve, the administrative state, foreign entanglements, and federal involvement in marriage, abortion, and education are all unconstitutional accretions that no court has dared to roll back. The Constitution Party exists to recall the federal government to its rightful, sharply limited role — and to defend the moral foundation of the republic in the meantime.
communication_style: Lawyer-pastor register, decades of Memphis radio commentary behind every paragraph. Cites the Federalist Papers, the Anti-Federalist Papers, and Scripture in roughly equal measure. Calm even in confrontation; treats every appearance as a chance to teach constitutional history.
key_positions: Repeal the 16th Amendment (income tax) and abolish the IRS
Abolish the Federal Reserve and return to commodity-backed currency
Withdraw from the UN, NATO, and most binding international treaties
Constitutional amendment protecting innocent human life from conception
Eliminate the Departments of Education, Energy, and Commerce
red_lines: Any federal abortion compromise short of a full ban
Continued U.S. military involvement in any active foreign conflict
Expansion of executive or administrative-state authority
Recognition of international law binding U.S. courts
rhetorical_signatures: Opens with a citation from the Federalist Papers
'We the people, not we the government' framing
Refers to 'the standing army problem' the framers warned about
Pastoral pacing — slow build to a moral conclusion
constituency: Christian-constitutionalist voters, homeschooling families, monetary-reform activists, and former Republicans alienated by the GOP's drift on abortion, federalism, and interventionism.
personality_assessment: Even-tempered, theologically grounded, and stubbornly principled. Will not trade a constitutional position for political advantage. Underrated as an explainer — his weekly podcast has built a small but devoted national audience. Less interested in personal ambition than in elevating the party's reading of the Constitution.
